title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · Relational Lens: Having Filter
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql having filter increment in an audited service-metrics query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.
Set result grain with GROUP BY and verify that every selected value is grouped or aggregated.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 3 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure. A defensible solution applies Having Filter, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an audited service-metrics query pack.

content.code:
WITH effort AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, SUM(minutes_spent) AS minutes
  FROM ticket_events
  GROUP BY ticket_id
)
SELECT teams.name, COUNT(tickets.ticket_id) AS tickets,
       COALESCE(SUM(effort.minutes), 0) AS minutes
FROM teams
LEFT JOIN tickets ON tickets.team_id = teams.team_id
LEFT JOIN effort ON effort.ticket_id = tickets.ticket_id
GROUP BY teams.team_id, teams.name
ORDER BY teams.name;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
